scope: Read-only local development Runner and adapter audit. No Worker or Pod was created and no provider credential was read.

runtime_catalog.disabled_reason: The local Loopal image did not satisfy its declared version probe and is absent from the catalog.

adapter_contract.runner_behavior: Runner uses CreatePodCommand.interaction_mode to choose ACP or generic PTY startup. ACP startup receives the Definition adapter_id; PTY startup does not infer or dispatch a per-adapter transport.
adapter_contract.attestation_limit: Runner InitializedConfirm reports only slug, binary version, and path. It does not attest adapter_id, supported interaction modes, or runtime-image digest.

deterministic_checks.meaning: The existing AgentFile evaluator materializes one selected JSON config bundle for OpenClaw.

deterministic_checks.command: go test selected non-Codex adapter packages -run 'Test.*(Register|Transport|AgentHome|MergeConfig)' -count=1 -timeout=90s

deterministic_checks.meaning: The Codex Definition adapter codex-app-server is registered without scanning local session history.
The Claude Definition adapter claude-stream-json has a runnable targeted transport test.
Generic PTY startup is covered for Aider, Hermes, MiniMax CLI, and OpenClaw's PTY transport class.
Every current formal Definition has zero or one configuration document. The AgentFile evaluator cannot yet safely represent a future Definition with multiple named configuration documents.

test_environment_defect.root_cause: The test passes an empty sandbox path. codexSessionDirs then includes the real ~/.codex/sessions directory, recursively scans user session JSONL files, and timed out while decoding local history.
test_environment_defect.scope_boundary: This is a test isolation defect outside the Worker creation contract. It was not modified during this audit and prevents using the full Codex adapter package test suite as release evidence.

support_conclusion: No Worker support status changed. Image availability, binary probe, connected Runner, adapter registration, or a focused evaluator test alone do not prove typed credential/config materialization, provider interaction, Pod lifecycle, terminal or ACP behavior, browser flow, cleanup, or release provenance.
